name: Haunted spaces
description: Films about demonic possession use architecture and environment to heighten fear. Late Night with the Devil places evil in a 1970s TV studio, connecting works where sets, buildings and media spaces shape horror, tension and supernatural menace.

key_work.title: Set design in Late Night with the Devil
key_work.brief_description:
Local horror film Late Night with the Devil (2023) is set on the fictional 1970s late-night talk show Night Owls with Jack Delroy. The film, shot as found footage during a Halloween special, follows spiritualists and sceptics – until things go horribly wrong, thanks to an uninvited supernatural guest.

Production Designer Otello Stolfo worked with the directors to accurately recreate the look of 1970s talk shows. From his original sketch shown here to the set’s palette of browns, oranges and blues, plus the patterns, finishes and dressings, Stolfo carefully recreated the style, tone and feel of Australian classics like The Don Lane Show (1975–83) and Countdown (1974–87), the latter of which he had worked on.

Filming in a Melbourne TV studio let the team use real floors and backstage areas, which added extra realism.

constellations_primary.name: Haunted spaces

start.title: The Shining
start.creator_credit: Stanley Kubrick and Producer Circle Company
start.brief_description: In Stanley Kubrick’s chilling adaptation of a Stephen King horror novel, Jack Torrance, a struggling writer, accepts a job as caretaker of the Overlook Hotel, a luxurious but remote resort with a macabre history of violence. The hotel is closed for the winter, and Jack, his wife, Wendy, and son, Danny, will be the only residents for several snowy months. Jack hopes that the solitude will give him a chance to focus on his writing, but he soon spirals helplessly towards madness and murder as the hotel’s power plays upon his weaknesses. Cast includes: Jack Nicholson, Shelley Duvall, Scatman Crothers and Danny Lloyd.

end.title: American Horror Story
end.creator_credit: Eryn Krueger Mekash, Evan Peters, Julie Herrin, Lou Eyrich, Todd Nenninger, 20th Century Fox Television, 20th Television, Brad Falchuk Teley-vision and Chloe Arbiture
end.brief_description: An anthology horror drama series centering on different characters and locations, including a house with a murderous past, an asylum, a witch coven, a freak show, a hotel, a farmhouse in Roanoke, a cult, the apocalypse and a summer camp.

constellations_other.name: Film and TV dreams
constellations_other.description: Fleetwood Mac's 'Dreams' coasted its way back up the charts 40 years after it was first released, thanks to Nathan Apodaca's joyous viral TikTok, but Fleetwood Mac (and Stevie Knicks) have been main staples of screen culture for decades.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Eryn Krueger Mekash, Evan Peters, Julie Herrin, Lou Eyrich, Todd Nenninger, 20th Century Fox Television, 20th Television, Brad Falchuk Teley-vision and Chloe Arbiture

links.description: Both works highlight hotels and professional spaces as vessels for horror. American Horror Story: Hotel emphasises how curated, public environments can become haunted, building tension through layout, lighting and narrative in ways reminiscent of The Shining.

end.title: Suspiria
end.creator_credit: Brad Fischer, David Kajganich, Francesco Melzi d'Eril, Gabriele Moratti, Luca Guadagnino, Marco Morabito, Silvia Venturini Fendi and William Sherak
end.headline_credit: Italy and United States of America, 2018
end.brief_description: A darkness swirls at the center of a world-renowned dance company, one that will engulf the troupe’s artistic director, an ambitious young dancer and a grieving psychotherapist. Some will succumb to the nightmare, others will finally wake up.

constellations_other.name: Breaking the binary
constellations_other.description: Tilda Swinton name has become synonymous with androgyny since she first appeared on our screens in the mid-1980s, leading a cultural shift towards today’s understanding of gender as fluid and ever-changing. Orlando was key to establishing her influence and allure.

constellations_other.name: Haunted spaces
constellations_other.description: Films about demonic possession use architecture and environment to heighten fear. Late Night with the Devil places evil in a 1970s TV studio, connecting works where sets, buildings and media spaces shape horror, tension and supernatural menace.

constellations_other.name: Antiques horrorshow
constellations_other.description: Rummage through this assortment of props and antiques in horror on screen, discovering valuable connections between them.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Brad Fischer, David Kajganich, Francesco Melzi d’Eril, Gabriele Moratti, Luca Guadagnino, Marco Morabito, Silvia Venturini Fendi and William Sherak

links.description: Dario Argento’s Suspiria sets a dance academy as a stylised, sinister environment. Like AHS‘ hotels, its architectural design amplifies the scares, showing how professional or artistic spaces can transform into theatrical stages for supernatural events and psychological unease.

end.title: Poltergeist
end.creator_credit: Frank Marshall, Steven Spielberg and Paul Huston
end.brief_description: One of the most famous horror movies of all time follows a family trying to deal with their haunted house by calling in parapsychologists. It’s also considered a cursed film because of tragedies associated with its production.

constellations_other.name: Haunted spaces
constellations_other.description: Films about demonic possession use architecture and environment to heighten fear. Late Night with the Devil places evil in a 1970s TV studio, connecting works where sets, buildings and media spaces shape horror, tension and supernatural menace.

constellations_other.name: Dragon's Lair
constellations_other.description: A look at pop-cultural interconnections that spawn from the lair of Don Bluth's arcade classic.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Frank Marshall, Steven Spielberg and Paul Huston

links.description: Poltergeist’s suburban home becomes a site of supernatural intrusion, with media technology acting as a portal for evil. Like Suspiria, the house is a carefully staged environment, demonstrating that horror can emerge when everyday spaces are destabilised.

end.title: Junji Ito Collection
end.creator_credit: Yuu Gotou, Asahi Shimbun Publications, Crunchyroll SC Anime Fund, Marvy Jack, Nippon Columbia, Pony Canyon Enterprise, SMIRAL Animation, Studio Deen and YTE
end.brief_description: A collection of animated horror stories based on the works of Japanese artist Junji Ito.

constellations_other.name: Haunted spaces
constellations_other.description: Films about demonic possession use architecture and environment to heighten fear. Late Night with the Devil places evil in a 1970s TV studio, connecting works where sets, buildings and media spaces shape horror, tension and supernatural menace.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Yuu Gotou, Asahi Shimbun Publications, Crunchyroll SC Anime Fund, Marvy Jack, Nippon Columbia, Pony Canyon Enterprise, SMIRAL Animation, Studio Deen and YTE

links.description: Ito’s horror adaptations frequently depict workplaces or communal spaces overtaken by supernatural forces. Like Poltergeist, these environments turn ordinary locations into terrifying arenas, showing how architecture, technology and layout can intensify fear and amplify demonic presence.

Local horror film Late Night with the Devil (2023) is set on the fictional 1970s late-night talk show Night Owls with Jack Delroy. end.title: The Exorcist
end.creator_credit: William Friedkin, William Peter Blatty and Warner Bros Pictures
end.brief_description: The Exorcist occupies a central position in the history of horror films, an almost sadistically malevolent experience in terror. Regan (Linda Blair) is a young girl entering puberty and she becomes possessed by the spirit of an ancient demon. Her mother (Ellen Burstyn) finds that there is no assistance in the scientific pragmatism of the modern world and she hesitantly turns to the occult rites of Catholicism and Orthodoxy in order to rid her daughter of the monster. The exorcism scenes attain a disturbing authenticity and they are based on the official Catholic Church rites of exorcism. “The Exorcist” is a film that feeds on our most intimate fears and terrors and not a film for the easily frightened. Stars Max Van Sydow as Father Merrin. Based on the book by William Peter Blatty. Mercedes McCambridge is the voice of the Devil. Part of Tubular Bell by Mike Oldfield used on the soundtrack.

constellations_other.name: Haunted spaces
constellations_other.description: Films about demonic possession use architecture and environment to heighten fear. Late Night with the Devil places evil in a 1970s TV studio, connecting works where sets, buildings and media spaces shape horror, tension and supernatural menace.

end.creator_credit_for_label: William Friedkin, William Peter Blatty and Warner Bros Pictures

links.description: While The Exorcist occurs in a domestic apartment, both films show ordinary spaces invaded by demonic forces. Confined environments - whether a TV studio or home - become crucibles for fear, concentrating tension and emphasising characters’ vulnerability to supernatural power.

end.title: The Shining
end.creator_credit: Stanley Kubrick and Producer Circle Company
end.brief_description: In Stanley Kubrick’s chilling adaptation of a Stephen King horror novel, Jack Torrance, a struggling writer, accepts a job as caretaker of the Overlook Hotel, a luxurious but remote resort with a macabre history of violence. The hotel is closed for the winter, and Jack, his wife, Wendy, and son, Danny, will be the only residents for several snowy months. Jack hopes that the solitude will give him a chance to focus on his writing, but he soon spirals helplessly towards madness and murder as the hotel’s power plays upon his weaknesses. constellations_other.name: Haunted spaces
constellations_other.description: Films about demonic possession use architecture and environment to heighten fear. Late Night with the Devil places evil in a 1970s TV studio, connecting works where sets, buildings and media spaces shape horror, tension and supernatural menace.

links.description: The Shining transforms the Overlook Hotel into a labyrinthine, oppressive space. Like The Exorcist, it shows how architecture reflects inner terror, demonstrating that hotels, apartments or studios can function as active participants in supernatural storytelling.
